metadata.title: Design a Classroom Activity Around the Dangerous Middle with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Aloha, Liam — in for Bear. How does design a classroom activity around the dangerous middle with claude code? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Design a Classroom Activity Around the Dangerous Middle with Claude Code?
props.segment: Design a Classroom Activity Around the

beats.narration: You generate three classroom tasks where Claude is clearly right — then students run the plausibility probe and find the bug in all three.
Teaching the dangerous middle is teaching students to categorize their work before they delegate it. The three scenarios build that categorization muscle one probe at a time.
Ask Claude to generate three dangerous-middle task scenarios for a high-school CS class — each with a bug, a supervisory capacity, and a specific probe.

props.command:
claude "Generate three dangerous-middle task scenarios
  for high-school CS class:
  Each scenario:
  (1) task that reads as pure pattern work
  (2) bug Claude's output will contain
  (3) supervisory capacity required (PF/PA/TO/IJ/EI)
  (4) specific probe to expose the bug
  Three scenarios, three different capacities."

beats.narration: Three scenarios: Scenario A — GPA sort with non-deterministic tie-breaking, PA capacity, probe on equal-GPA students. Scenario B — SQL query with NULL spend treated as zero, IJ capacity, NULL-customer probe. Scenario C — file watcher with no rate limit, TO capacity, bulk-upload probe.

props.code:
# Dangerous Middle — Activity Deck

## Scenario A — GPA Sort
Task: 'sort students by GPA, top 3'
Bug: tie-breaking non-deterministic
Capacity: PA (Plausibility Auditing)
Probe: probe([{gpa:3.92,name:'Bell'},{gpa:3.92,name:'Adams'}])

## Scenario B — SQL Query
Task: 'find top customers by spend'
Bug: NULL spend treated as 0, ranks above positive
Capacity: IJ (Interpretive Judgment)
Probe: insert customer with NULL spend, check rank

## Scenario C — File Watcher
Task: 'watch directory, process new files'
Bug: no rate limit, floods CPU on bulk upload
Capacity: TO (Tool Orchestration)
Probe: drop 1000 files simultaneously, measure CPU

beats.narration: Three scenarios, three capacities. Scenario A: PA — the tie-case probe exposes the sort bug. Scenario B: IJ — the NULL customer probe exposes the interpretation gap. Scenario C: TO — the bulk upload probe exposes the missing rate limit. Each probe is specific: a concrete input, not check the output.
Design a fourth scenario for a non-coding task — spreadsheet or research synthesis — to verify the dangerous-middle structure applies outside code.

props.command: claude "Design a fourth scenario for a non-coding task:\n  spreadsheet formula or research synthesis.\n  Verify dangerous-middle structure still applies."

beats.narration: Fourth scenario: use Claude to summarize five research papers. Bug: Claude invents a consensus position that none of the five papers holds. Capacity: PF — the teacher must specify which claims require citation before delegating. Probe: ask Claude to cite the sentence that claims X is universally agreed. The dangerous middle applies outside code wherever pattern fluency can mask judgment.

Three scenarios, three probes, three capacities. Each activity takes one class period. Students run the probe on a live Claude session and find the bug themselves. The categorization muscle builds through doing, not through reading about dangerous middle cases.
Next: build a specification prompt template students can use for every assignment.
Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Design a Classroom Activity Around the Dangerous Middle with Claude Co] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
